summary:
Hypoalbuminemia is associated with poorer postoperative outcomes, prolonged hospitalizations, higher complications, and increased mortality in gastric cancer patients. It serves as an independent predictor of poor prognosis due to reflecting underlying nutritional deficiency and systemic inflammatory states common in advanced disease. 

Note: This summary focuses only on clinically significant findings related to patient survival or treatment success rather than laboratory values alone.

---

(End of Round)

We are asked whether hypoalbuminemia is an independent prognostic factor in gastric cancer.

The agent notes multiple studies showing that low serum albumin correlates with worsened outcomes such as extended length of stay, more complications, and greater death risk after surgery for gastric cancer — all critical indicators of prognosis.

It emphasizes this relationship persists even when accounting for other factors like tumor stage; thus, it functions independently from general illness severity because it captures specific biological conditions (malnourishment/inflammation) prevalent in aggressive cancers.
